title: Smoky Roasted Saudi-Arabian Beef Tagine
description: A resinous, snappy, nostalgic Saudi-Arabian dish featuring ground beef, smoke-cold, and Ayurvedic-tamasic-friendly ingredients.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the onion and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic, cumin, turmeric, cinnamon, and ginger, and cook for 1 minute.
Add the ground beef and cook until browned, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Add the smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Transfer the mixture to a large bowl and set aside.
In a small bowl, whisk together the pomegranate molasses and 1 cup of water.
Pour the pomegranate mixture over the meat mixture and stir to combine.
Transfer the mixture to a 9x13-inch baking dish and roast in the preheated oven for 30 minutes.
Stir the mixture and continue to roast for an additional 20 minutes.
Garnish with fresh parsley and serve hot.
Let the tagine rest for 10 minutes before slicing and serving.
Slice the tagine and serve with additional pomegranate molasses, if desired.
Refrigerate or freeze leftovers for up to 3 days.
Reheat the tagine in the oven or on the stovetop until hot and steaming.
Serve hot with naan bread or over rice.
